tests.scope: Confirm every input-validation guard added to the four user-side RS-request wrappers (create_user_rs_request, get_user_rs_request, cancel_user_rs_request, perform_user_rs_request_action) raises AlmaValidationError BEFORE any HTTP call is issued. Exercises: create_user_rs_request on empty/None/non-string user_id, on empty/None/non-dict request_data; get_user_rs_request on empty/None user_id and empty/None request_id; cancel_user_rs_request on empty/None user_id, empty/None request_id, and non-string reason (note: per the shipped signature reason is OPTIONAL on the RS DELETE — only the non-string-when-supplied guard is asserted, NOT a 'reason required' guard); perform_user_rs_request_action on empty/None user_id, empty/None request_id, and empty/None/non-string op. Pure runtime guard exercise; no SANDBOX state is read or mutated. The test proves the guards are wired in the build the operator will actually run, complementing the chunk's mocked-HTTP unit tests under tests/unit/domains/test_users.py.

tests.scope: Read-only SANDBOX smoke for get_user_rs_request. The user-side RS endpoints have NO list verb (the swagger only exposes create / get / cancel / perform-action on a specific request_id), so this test takes a pre-existing RS request_id supplied by the operator (typically the request_id captured from t-42-3's create step on a previous run, or a long-lived RS request the operator knows exists on the test user) and calls users.get_user_rs_request(${existing_user_primary_id}, ${existing_rs_request_id}) to confirm the wrapper round-trips the GET endpoint and returns a non-empty dict. If the operator has no pre-existing RS request to point at, this test is skipped at runtime by the operator (the round-trip in t-42-3 already exercises the GET path mid-flight, so skipping t-42-2 does not leave the GET endpoint unexercised). No state is mutated.

tests.scope: Operator-authorized live round-trip: create a real user-side resource-sharing request on ${existing_user_primary_id} (the request body uses synthetic placeholder bibliographic metadata so the test does not depend on any specific MMS ID), GET it back to confirm the request_id round-trips, perform the swagger-documented 'update_shipping' action with a synthetic ${shipping_cost} and ${fund_code} to exercise perform_user_rs_request_action against live SANDBOX, then CANCEL the request via DELETE with the documented cleanup reason 'AUTOMATED_REGRESSION_TEST_CLEANUP' and notify_user=False to avoid spamming the test user's email. Final step: re-GET to confirm the request is no longer fetchable as an active request (Alma either 404s or moves the request to a terminal status; either way the wrapper raises AlmaAPIError). The cancel step is the cleanup happy-path - the finally block only retries if it did not run or failed. The round-trip exercises all four wrapper methods against live SANDBOX. The test prints request_id, user_id, and the RS-library owner at runtime; if the cancel step fails the operator must cancel the leftover RS request via the Alma staff UI (the 'AUTOMATED_REGRESSION_TEST_CLEANUP' reason makes it unambiguous). Net state on a happy run: the user's RS-request history shows one created/cancelled audit artifact, similar to the t-41-3 hold-request audit pattern. NOTE: this test requires (a) the test user be affiliated with a resource-sharing library and (b) the operator supply the resource-sharing library code as ${rs_library_code}; if either is missing, the wrapper will raise AlmaAPIError (codes 401607 / 401768) and the test will fail loud - this is the intended signal to the operator that the SANDBOX RS setup is incomplete.

needsHumanInput.description: An existing Alma user_primary_id in SANDBOX that the operator has authorized for read/write resource-sharing-request smoke testing. The user must be affiliated with a resource-sharing library (per the chunk's documented Alma error 401768). Used by t-42-2 (read-only get) and t-42-3 (live create -> get -> update-shipping -> cancel RS-request round-trip with in-band cancel-as-cleanup). Synthetic placeholder only - the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime; never write a real user_primary_id into this file.

The Alma resource-sharing library code that ${existing_user_primary_id} is affiliated with (the 'owner' field in the create_user_rs_request body). Required by Alma per error code 401607 ('Resource sharing library (owner) is missing'). The operator supplies the SANDBOX RS-library code; the bib metadata in the body is otherwise synthetic. Synthetic placeholder only - the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime; never write a real library code into this file.

A non-negative shipping-cost value (as a string, per the swagger) used by the perform_user_rs_request_action(op='update_shipping') step. Any small positive value is acceptable; '0.00' also works. The value is round-tripped through Alma and does not affect cleanup. Synthetic placeholder only - the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime.

An Alma fund code in SANDBOX that the test user / RS library is permitted to bill against (used by perform_user_rs_request_action(op='update_shipping')). Round-tripped through Alma; does not affect cleanup. Synthetic placeholder only - the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime; never write a real fund code into this file.

pythonCalls: from almaapitk.domains.users import Users
from almaapitk.client.AlmaAPIClient import AlmaAPIError, AlmaValidationError
users = Users(client)

create_response = None
create_data = None
request_id = None
get_response = None
get_request_id_echo = None
action_response = None
cancel_response = None
post_cancel_error = None
print(f'[t-42-3] live create -> get -> update_shipping -> cancel RS request on user ${existing_user_primary_id}, owner ${rs_library_code}')

create_data = getattr(create_response, 'data', None) if create_response is not None else None
if isinstance(create_data, dict):
    request_id = create_data.get('request_id') or create_data.get('id')
print(f'[t-42-3] RS request created: request_id={request_id!r} user_id=${existing_user_primary_id} owner=${rs_library_code}')

# Exercise perform_user_rs_request_action with the swagger-documented
# op='update_shipping' before cancelling. Failure here is non-fatal to
# cleanup: even if Alma rejects the update (e.g., the request is not
# yet in the right workflow state), the cancel step below still runs.
try:
    action_response = users.perform_user_rs_request_action(
        '${existing_user_primary_id}',
        str(request_id),
        op='update_shipping',
        shipping_cost='${shipping_cost}',
        fund_code='${fund_code}',
    ) if request_id else None
except AlmaAPIError as e:
    print(f'[t-42-3] update_shipping action returned API error (non-fatal, proceeding to cancel): {e}')
    action_response = e

# In-band cancel: DELETE with the documented cleanup reason and
# notify_user=False so the test user is not emailed. This IS the
# cleanup happy-path - the finally block only retries if this step
# did not run or failed.
cancel_response = users.cancel_user_rs_request(
    '${existing_user_primary_id}',
    str(request_id),
    reason='AUTOMATED_REGRESSION_TEST_CLEANUP',
    notify_user=False,
) if request_id else None
print(f'[t-42-3] RS request cancelled: request_id={request_id!r} status={getattr(cancel_response, "status_code", None)} success={getattr(cancel_response, "success", None)}')

# Confirm the request is no longer an active fetchable RS request.
# Alma either returns 404 (gone) or marks it CANCELLED; the wrapper
# raises AlmaAPIError either way on a non-active fetch.
try:
    users.get_user_rs_request('${existing_user_primary_id}', str(request_id))
except AlmaAPIError as e:
    post_cancel_error = e
passCriteria: create_response is not None and getattr(create_response, 'success', False) is True
isinstance(create_data, dict) and request_id is not None
cancel_response is not None and getattr(cancel_response, 'success', False) is True
cleanup:
# Belt-and-braces: if the in-band cancel did not run (e.g. an
# earlier assertion raised) or did not succeed, retry the cancel
# with the same documented reason. If it still fails, print a
# manual-cleanup banner with request_id, user_id, and owner so the
# operator can cancel the leftover RS request via the Alma staff
# UI. Do NOT re-raise from finally - let the original test failure
# surface.
try:
    if request_id and (cancel_response is None or not getattr(cancel_response, 'success', False)):
        users2 = _UsersCleanup(client)
        users2.cancel_user_rs_request(
            '${existing_user_primary_id}',
            str(request_id),
            reason='AUTOMATED_REGRESSION_TEST_CLEANUP',
            notify_user=False,
        )
        print(f'[t-42-3 cleanup] in-finally cancel succeeded for request_id={request_id!r}')
except AlmaAPIError as cleanup_err:
    print(f'\n!!! [t-42-3] MANUAL CLEANUP REQUIRED !!!\n!!! request_id: {request_id!r}\n!!! user_id: ${existing_user_primary_id}\n!!! owner (RS library): ${rs_library_code}\n!!! reason: {cleanup_err}\n!!! Operator must cancel the leftover RS request via the Alma staff UI.\n')

tests.scope: Read-only SANDBOX smoke for list_user_purchase_requests. Calls users.list_user_purchase_requests(${existing_user_primary_id}) to confirm the GET /users/{user_id}/purchase-requests endpoint round-trips and returns a flat List[Dict] envelope (the wrapper unwraps Alma's 'user_request' / 'purchase_request' envelope key). The test user is likely to have zero purchase requests on a fresh fixture, in which case the list is empty and that is still a pass for the list-shape assertion. If the returned list is non-empty, picks the first request's id and calls get_user_purchase_request(${existing_user_primary_id}, <id>) to confirm the single-request GET round-trips and returns a non-empty Dict[str, Any]. If the list is empty, the get-step is skipped - the test is still considered pass because the list endpoint itself responded correctly. No state is mutated. Covers the read half of issue #43's integration coverage; the create -> get -> cancel live cycle is exercised by t-43-3.

tests.scope: Operator-authorized live round-trip: create a real user-side purchase request on ${existing_user_primary_id} with synthetic placeholder bibliographic metadata (no specific MMS ID required - the request body carries title / author / format only), GET it back to confirm the purchase_request_id round-trips, list_user_purchase_requests to confirm the new request appears in the user's list, and then CANCEL it via perform_user_purchase_request_action(op='cancel') - the swagger-documented cancellation path for purchase requests (there is NO separate DELETE endpoint per the shipped wrapper's docstring). The cancel step is the cleanup happy-path - the finally block only retries if it did not run or failed. The round-trip exercises all four wrapper methods against live SANDBOX (create / get / list / perform-action). The test prints purchase_request_id and user_id at runtime; if the cancel step fails the operator must mark the leftover purchase request as cancelled via the Alma staff UI (the 'AlmaAPITK regression-smoke purchase request' title makes it unambiguous). Net state on a happy run: the user's purchase-request history shows one created/cancelled request as an audit artifact.

pythonCalls: from almaapitk.domains.users import Users
from almaapitk.client.AlmaAPIClient import AlmaAPIError, AlmaValidationError
users = Users(client)

create_response = None
create_data = None
purchase_request_id = None
get_response = None
get_pr_id_echo = None
list_after_create = None
found_in_list = False
cancel_response = None
cancel_succeeded = False
print(f'[t-43-3] live create -> get -> list -> cancel purchase request on user ${existing_user_primary_id}')

create_data = getattr(create_response, 'data', None) if create_response is not None else None
if isinstance(create_data, dict):
    purchase_request_id = create_data.get('id') or create_data.get('purchase_request_id')
print(f'[t-43-3] purchase request created: purchase_request_id={purchase_request_id!r} user_id=${existing_user_primary_id}')

# In-band cancel via the swagger-documented op='cancel' action. This
# IS the cleanup happy-path - the finally block only retries if this
# step did not run or failed. Purchase requests have NO DELETE verb
# per the shipped wrapper's docstring; cancellation is the op path.
cancel_response = users.perform_user_purchase_request_action(
    '${existing_user_primary_id}',
    str(purchase_request_id),
    op='cancel',
) if purchase_request_id else None
cancel_succeeded = bool(getattr(cancel_response, 'success', False)) if cancel_response is not None else False
print(f'[t-43-3] purchase request cancelled: purchase_request_id={purchase_request_id!r} status={getattr(cancel_response, "status_code", None)} success={cancel_succeeded}')
passCriteria: create_response is not None and getattr(create_response, 'success', False) is True
isinstance(create_data, dict) and purchase_request_id is not None
isinstance(list_after_create, list) and found_in_list is True
cancel_response is not None and cancel_succeeded is True
cleanup:
# Belt-and-braces: if the in-band cancel did not run (e.g. an
# earlier assertion raised) or did not succeed, retry the cancel
# via perform_user_purchase_request_action(op='cancel'). If it
# still fails, print a manual-cleanup banner with the
# purchase_request_id and user_id so the operator can mark the
# leftover purchase request as cancelled via the Alma staff UI.
# Do NOT re-raise from finally - let the original test failure
# surface.
try:
    if purchase_request_id and not cancel_succeeded:
        users2 = _UsersCleanup(client)
        users2.perform_user_purchase_request_action(
            '${existing_user_primary_id}',
            str(purchase_request_id),
            op='cancel',
        )
        print(f'[t-43-3 cleanup] in-finally cancel succeeded for purchase_request_id={purchase_request_id!r}')
except AlmaAPIError as cleanup_err:
    print(f'\n!!! [t-43-3] MANUAL CLEANUP REQUIRED !!!\n!!! purchase_request_id: {purchase_request_id!r}\n!!! user_id: ${existing_user_primary_id}\n!!! reason: {cleanup_err}\n!!! Operator must cancel the leftover purchase request via the Alma staff UI (title: AlmaAPITK regression-smoke purchase request).\n')
